page: Microsoft Azure M416-208s_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel Xeon 8280L processors, delivering 208 dedicated vCPUs across 104 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled. The system operates at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz and is equipped with 5700.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 27.4 GB of memory per physical core. Local storage consists of 8389 GB of Premium Storage-capable capacity, while no complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data highlights a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. While single-core metrics are relatively low, with stress-ng single-core performance and gzip compression falling into the bottom 25%, the server excels in parallelized workloads. It achieves top-tier status in multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, with cached read bandwidth reaching 10446.46 GB/sec and uncached read/write bandwidths of 651.24 GB/sec and 347.19 GB/sec respectively, both placing in the top 10%. Redis SET operations also perform at a top-tier level, executing 162.62 million operations per second.

Given its massive memory footprint and high vCPU count, this server represents a high-density hardware class designed for enterprise-scale workloads. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-threaded efficiency, meaning applications relying on single-core speed will not utilize this hardware optimally. However, for memory-intensive, highly parallel applications, the resource density offers significant qualitative cost efficiency by consolidating massive datasets into a single node. It is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores like Redis, heavy data warehousing, and complex multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure M416-208s_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MSv2 family, designed for massive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor with 208 dedicated vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, it features 5700.0 GB of RAM and 8389 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks fall into lower percentiles, its multi-core and memory subsystems deliver top-tier performance. It achieves exceptional multi-core stress-ng scores, top-tier memory read/write bandwidth, and outstanding Redis SET throughput at 162.62 million operations per second. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and highly parallelized enterprise applications where massive memory capacity and high memory bandwidth are critical.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M416-208s_v2 is an MSv2-family virtual machine featuring 208 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 5.7 TB of RAM, and 8.3 TB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M416-208s_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 208 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 5700 GB RAM, and 8389 GB storage, built for large-scale databases.
tagline: A massive memory-optimized virtual machine delivering top-tier multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth for enterprise-scale in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 208 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 5700 GB of RAM
8389 GB of Premium Storage-capable local capacity
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Executes 162.62 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and data warehousing
Lower single-core efficiency offset by massive parallel processing power
